runtimeEvidence: Snapshot-mode inventoryShipmentCreate stages a draft shipment over existing product-backed inventory items and records the raw mutation for commit replay.
inventoryShipmentCreateInTransit and inventoryShipmentMarkInTransit increase the first effective inventory level's incoming quantity for unreceived shipment quantities.
inventoryShipmentAddItems, inventoryShipmentRemoveItems, and inventoryShipmentUpdateItemQuantities update shipment line totals and adjust incoming inventory atomically for in-transit shipments.
inventoryShipmentSetTracking stages trackingNumber, company, trackingUrl, and arrivesAt without upstream writes.
inventoryShipmentReceive moves accepted quantities from incoming to available and on_hand, reduces incoming for rejected quantities, and updates shipment status to PARTIALLY_RECEIVED or RECEIVED.
inventoryShipmentDelete tombstones local draft/in-transit shipments, reverses remaining incoming inventory, and makes inventoryShipment(id:) return null.
Invalid inventory item ids, unknown shipment ids, invalid quantities, and invalid status transitions return local userErrors without contacting Shopify.
documentedBlockers: movementId is preserved as shipment metadata only; transfer/movement routing, origin/destination topology, and shipment name allocation are not modeled until a dedicated inventory transfer state model exists.

Support is intentionally scoped to product-backed inventory items and the first effective inventory level. Multi-location routing and non-product inventory owners need separate conformance evidence before broadening.
commitReplayEvidence: The runtime tests assert staged shipment mutations are appended to the meta mutation log in original request order with original raw GraphQL bodies.
